message:
test(conformance): add cross-runtime comparator, mock layer, and diff tests

New test infrastructure for differential extension conformance testing
(comparing TS oracle output against Rust QuickJS runtime):

- conformance_comparator.rs (1,115 lines, bd-2ylf): semantic JSON
  comparator handling key ordering, float precision (1e-10 tolerance),
  null-vs-missing equivalence, set-based array comparison for
  registrations, ordered comparison for hostcall sequences
- conformance_mock.rs (924 lines, bd-1bje): mock session layer
  implementing ExtensionSession with HostcallCaptureLog for recording
  all hostcall invocations during conformance runs
- ext_conformance_diff.rs (566 lines): differential tests that load
  extensions through both Rust swc+QuickJS and TS oracle (Bun+jiti)
  pipelines, then compare registration snapshots
- ext_conformance_generated.rs (912 lines): generated conformance
  test cases from the artifact corpus
- mock_spec_schema.rs (70 lines): JSON Schema validation of mock spec
  fixtures against docs/schema/mock_spec.json using jsonschema crate
- ts_oracle/batch_load.sh (72 lines): bash script to batch-load all
  official extensions through the TS oracle harness with pass/fail
  summary output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+#!/bin/bash
+# Batch-load all official extensions through the TS oracle harness.
+# Outputs a summary of pass/fail for each extension.
+#
+# Usage: bash batch_load.sh [artifacts-dir]
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"
+ARTIFACTS="${1:-$SCRIPT_DIR/../artifacts}"
+HARNESS="$SCRIPT_DIR/load_extension.ts"
+PI_MONO_ROOT="$SCRIPT_DIR/../../../legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono"
+BUN="/home/ubuntu/.bun/bin/bun"
+
+export NODE_PATH="$PI_MONO_ROOT/node_modules"
+
+passed=0
+failed=0
+errors=()
+
+# Find all single-file extensions (*.ts in artifacts root dirs)
+for ext_dir in "$ARTIFACTS"/*/; do
+    dir_name=$(basename "$ext_dir")
+
+    # Skip non-extension directories
+    [[ "$dir_name" == "community" ]] && continue
+    [[ "$dir_name" == "npm-registry" ]] && continue
+    [[ "$dir_name" == "third-party" ]] && continue
+    [[ "$dir_name" == "agents-"* ]] && continue
+
+    # Find the entry point
+    entry=""
+    if [[ -f "$ext_dir/index.ts" ]]; then
+        entry="$ext_dir/index.ts"
+    elif [[ -f "$ext_dir/$dir_name.ts" ]]; then
+        entry="$ext_dir/$dir_name.ts"
+    else
+        # Look for any single .ts file
+        ts_files=("$ext_dir"/*.ts)
+        if [[ ${#ts_files[@]} -eq 1 && -f "${ts_files[0]}" ]]; then
+            entry="${ts_files[0]}"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$entry" ]]; then
+        echo "SKIP  $dir_name (no entry point found)"
+        continue
+    fi
+
+    # Run the harness with a timeout
+    result=$(timeout 15 "$BUN" run "$HARNESS" "$entry" /tmp 2>/dev/null || echo '{"success": false, "error": "timeout or crash"}')
+
+    success=$(echo "$result" | grep -o '"success": *[a-z]*' | head -1 | grep -o 'true\|false')
+
+    if [[ "$success" == "true" ]]; then
+        echo "PASS  $dir_name"
+        ((passed++))
+    else
+        error=$(echo "$result" | grep -o '"error": "[^"]*"' | head -1 | sed 's/"error": "//;s/"$//')
+        echo "FAIL  $dir_name: ${error:0:80}"
+        ((failed++))
+        errors+=("$dir_name")
+    fi
+done
+
+echo ""
+echo "=== Summary ==="
+echo "Passed: $passed"
+echo "Failed: $failed"
+if [[ ${#errors[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+    echo "Failed extensions: ${errors[*]}"
+fi
